text.title: usability-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for usability-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Helping a user who is stuck by pointing at the correct area or saying "try the menu".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any help makes the finding invalid. Record the failure; do not rescue it. The user's struggle is the finding.

failure-mode.description: Recruiting participants who do not match the target user profile (e.g., testing a parenting app with childless users).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Results reflect the recruited population, not the actual user base. Wrong participants produce misleading findings.

failure-mode.description: Collecting only qualitative observations without any quantitative metrics (no task success rates, no time-on-task).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Purely subjective findings are difficult to prioritize and impossible to track over time. Always measure: task success (binary: completed without help), time on task, and satisfaction (numeric scale).

failure-mode.description: Fixing issues mid-study ("We noticed this bug in session 1, let's fix it before session 2").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fixing mid-study invalidates comparative data across sessions. Pause recruiting, fix the bug, then resume with clear documentation of the intervention point. Or accept the inconsistency and note it in the report.
